lines.english: Lesson 29
lines.chinese: 第29课
lines.raw: Lesson 29 | 第29课

lines.english: Funny or not?
lines.chinese: 好笑与否？
lines.raw: Funny or not? | 好笑与否？

lines.english: What is the basis of 'sick' humour?
lines.chinese: “病态”幽默的基础是什么？
lines.raw: What is the basis of 'sick' humour? | “病态”幽默的基础是什么？

lines.english: Whether we find a joke funny or not largely depends on where we have been brought up.
lines.chinese: 我们觉得一个笑话好不好笑，在很大程度上取决于我们是在哪里长大的。
lines.raw: Whether we find a joke funny or not largely depends on where we have been brought up. | 我们觉得一个笑话好不好笑，在很大程度上取决于我们是在哪里长大的。

lines.english: The sense of humour is mysteriously bound up with national characteristics.
lines.chinese: 幽默感与民族特点有着神秘的联系。
lines.raw: The sense of humour is mysteriously bound up with national characteristics. | 幽默感与民族特点有着神秘的联系。

lines.english: A Frenchman, for instance, might find it hard to laugh at a Russian joke.
lines.chinese: 例如，法国人可能觉得俄罗斯笑话不好笑。
lines.raw: A Frenchman, for instance, might find it hard to laugh at a Russian joke. | 例如，法国人可能觉得俄罗斯笑话不好笑。

lines.english: In the same way, a Russian might fail to see anything amusing in a joke
lines.chinese: 同样，俄罗斯人可能觉得一个笑话没什么可乐的，
lines.raw: In the same way, a Russian might fail to see anything amusing in a joke | 同样，俄罗斯人可能觉得一个笑话没什么可乐的，

lines.english: which would make an Englishman laugh to tears.
lines.chinese: 而这个笑话却能让英国人捧腹大笑。
lines.raw: which would make an Englishman laugh to tears. | 而这个笑话却能让英国人捧腹大笑。

lines.english: Most funny stories are based on comic situations.
lines.chinese: 大多数有趣的故事都基于滑稽的情境。
lines.raw: Most funny stories are based on comic situations. | 大多数有趣的故事都基于滑稽的情境。

lines.english: In spite of national differences, certain funny situations have a universal appeal.
lines.chinese: 尽管存在民族差异，但某些滑稽情境具有普遍的吸引力。
lines.raw: In spite of national differences, certain funny situations have a universal appeal. | 尽管存在民族差异，但某些滑稽情境具有普遍的吸引力。

lines.english: No matter where you live,
lines.chinese: 无论你住在哪里，
lines.raw: No matter where you live, | 无论你住在哪里，

lines.english: you would find it difficult not to laugh at, say, Charlie Chaplin's early films.
lines.chinese: 你都会觉得很难不对，比如说，查理·卓别林的早期电影发笑。
lines.raw: you would find it difficult not to laugh at, say, Charlie Chaplin's early films. | 你都会觉得很难不对，比如说，查理·卓别林的早期电影发笑。

lines.english: However, a new type of humour, which stems largely from U.S., has recently come into fashion.
lines.chinese: 然而，一种主要源自美国的幽默新类型，最近流行起来。
lines.raw: However, a new type of humour, which stems largely from U.S., has recently come into fashion. | 然而，一种主要源自美国的幽默新类型，最近流行起来。

lines.english: It is called 'sick humour'. Comedians base their jokes on tragic situations like violent death or serious accidents.
lines.chinese: 它被称为“病态幽默”。喜剧演员将他们的笑话建立在悲剧情境上，例如暴力死亡或严重事故。
lines.raw: It is called 'sick humour'. Comedians base their jokes on tragic situations like violent death or serious accidents. | 它被称为“病态幽默”。喜剧演员将他们的笑话建立在悲剧情境上，例如暴力死亡或严重事故。

lines.english: Many people find this sort of joke distasteful.
lines.chinese: 许多人觉得这种笑话令人反感。
lines.raw: Many people find this sort of joke distasteful. | 许多人觉得这种笑话令人反感。

lines.english: The following example of 'sick humour' will enable you to judge for yourself.
lines.chinese: 下面这个“病态幽默”的例子能让你自己评判。
lines.raw: The following example of 'sick humour' will enable you to judge for yourself. | 下面这个“病态幽默”的例子能让你自己评判。

lines.english: A man who had broken his right leg was taken to hospital a few weeks before Christmas.
lines.chinese: 一个右腿骨折的男人在圣诞节前几周被送进了医院。
lines.raw: A man who had broken his right leg was taken to hospital a few weeks before Christmas. | 一个右腿骨折的男人在圣诞节前几周被送进了医院。

lines.english: From the moment he arrived there,
lines.chinese: 从他到达那里那一刻起，
lines.raw: From the moment he arrived there, | 从他到达那里那一刻起，

lines.english: he kept on pestering his doctor to tell him when he would be able to go home.
lines.chinese: 他就一直缠着医生，问他什么时候能回家。
lines.raw: he kept on pestering his doctor to tell him when he would be able to go home. | 他就一直缠着医生，问他什么时候能回家。

lines.english: He dreaded having to spend Christmas in hospital.
lines.chinese: 他害怕不得不在医院过圣诞节。
lines.raw: He dreaded having to spend Christmas in hospital. | 他害怕不得不在医院过圣诞节。

lines.english: Though the doctor did his best, the patient's recovery was slow.
lines.chinese: 尽管医生尽了力，但病人的康复很慢。
lines.raw: Though the doctor did his best, the patient's recovery was slow. | 尽管医生尽了力，但病人的康复很慢。

lines.english: On Christmas day, the man still had his right leg in plaster.
lines.chinese: 圣诞节那天，这个男人右腿上仍然打着石膏。
lines.raw: On Christmas day, the man still had his right leg in plaster. | 圣诞节那天，这个男人右腿上仍然打着石膏。

lines.english: He spent a miserable day in bed thinking of all the fun he was missing.
lines.chinese: 他在床上度过了悲惨的一天，想着自己错过了所有的乐趣。
lines.raw: He spent a miserable day in bed thinking of all the fun he was missing. | 他在床上度过了悲惨的一天，想着自己错过了所有的乐趣。

lines.english: The following day, however, the doctor consoled him by telling him
lines.chinese: 然而，第二天，医生安慰他说，
lines.raw: The following day, however, the doctor consoled him by telling him | 然而，第二天，医生安慰他说，

lines.english: that his chances of being able to leave hospital in time for New Year celebrations were good.
lines.chinese: 他赶在新年庆祝活动前出院的可能性很大。
lines.raw: that his chances of being able to leave hospital in time for New Year celebrations were good. | 他赶在新年庆祝活动前出院的可能性很大。

lines.english: The man took heart and sure enough, on New Year's Eve he was able to hobble along to a party.
lines.chinese: 男人振作起来，果然，在除夕夜他能一瘸一拐地去参加派对。
lines.raw: The man took heart and sure enough, on New Year's Eve he was able to hobble along to a party. | 男人振作起来，果然，在除夕夜他能一瘸一拐地去参加派对。

lines.english: To compensate for his unpleasant experiences in hospital,
lines.chinese: 为了弥补他在医院的不愉快经历，
lines.raw: To compensate for his unpleasant experiences in hospital, | 为了弥补他在医院的不愉快经历，

lines.english: the man drank a little more than was good for him.
lines.chinese: 他多喝了一点酒，这对他身体不好。
lines.raw: the man drank a little more than was good for him. | 他多喝了一点酒，这对他身体不好。

lines.english: In the process, he enjoyed himself thoroughly and kept telling everybody how much he hated hospitals.
lines.chinese: 在此过程中，他玩得很开心，并且不停地告诉所有人他有多么讨厌医院。
lines.raw: In the process, he enjoyed himself thoroughly and kept telling everybody how much he hated hospitals. | 在此过程中，他玩得很开心，并且不停地告诉所有人他有多么讨厌医院。

lines.english: He was still mumbling something about hospitals at the end of the party when he slipped on a piece of ice and broke his left leg.
lines.chinese: 派对结束时，他还在嘟囔着关于医院的事情，这时他滑到了一块冰上，摔断了左腿。
lines.raw: He was still mumbling something about hospitals at the end of the party when he slipped on a piece of ice and broke his left leg. | 派对结束时，他还在嘟囔着关于医院的事情，这时他滑到了一块冰上，摔断了左腿。
